name: Vector Higgs-portal dark matter
aliases: vector boson dark matter via Higgs
summary: A new dark gauge boson V_mu (Abelian or hidden non-Abelian) couples to the SM Higgs through the gauge-invariant operator lambda_v |H|^2 V_mu V^mu. Vector mass m_V emerges from electroweak-vev contribution m_V^2 = lambda_v v^2 + m_V,0^2, and Higgs-portal couplings drive thermal freeze-out giving correct relic abundance Omega_V h^2 ~ 0.12 for m_V ~ 0.1-1 TeV with lambda_v ~ 0.01-0.1. Predicts Higgs invisible decay h -> V V if m_V < m_h/2 and direct-detection cross-section sigma_SI ~ 10^-46 cm^2 for m_V ~ 100 GeV. LZ 2024 direct-detection bounds exclude a swath of (lambda_v, m_V) space; m_V ~ 60-65 GeV (near m_h/2 resonance dip) survives.

addresses_puzzles.note: Standard thermal-WIMP-via-Higgs-portal with vector mediator instead of scalar (higgs_portal_scalar_dm). Direct-detection bound from LZ 2024 (sigma_SI < 9.2e-48 cm^2 at 30 GeV) excludes most of the natural parameter space; survives in resonant region m_V ~ m_h/2 and in heavy-mass tail m_V > 1 TeV.

touches_benchmarks.note: Most natural parameter space excluded; survives in resonant (m_V ~ m_h/2 ~ 62 GeV) and heavy (m_V > TeV) regions.
Higgs invisible decay BR(h -> VV) ~ 5-10% if kinematically open; constrains lambda_v through Higgs coupling fits and invisible-width searches.

new_symmetries: hidden U(1) or non-Abelian gauge symmetry
introduces.note: Vector mass and Higgs-portal coupling; or equivalently, gauge coupling and dark-Higgs vev if V acquires mass via dark Higgs mechanism.

history: Hambye (2009), 'Hidden vector dark matter', JHEP 01, 028, arXiv:0811.0172 - foundational. Vector Higgs-portal analyses: Lebedev, Lee, Mambrini (2012), 'Vector Higgs-portal dark matter and the invisible Higgs', Phys. Lett. B 707, 570, arXiv:1111.4482. Yaguna (2009); Djouadi-Falkowski-Mambrini-Quevillon (2013), arXiv:1205.3169. Updated LZ-constraint analyses: Arcadi-Djouadi-Raidal (2020, arXiv:2105.00010).

references.citation: Lebedev, Lee, Mambrini, 'Vector Higgs-portal dark matter and the invisible Higgs', Phys. Lett. B 707, 570 (2012)
